questions.questionText: Under the Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme (RNTCP/NTEP), the recommended treatment regimen for new drug-sensitive pulmonary TB is:
questions.optionB: 2 months HRZE (isoniazid, rifampicin, pyrazinamide, ethambutol) + 4 months HRE — daily fixed-dose combination under DOTS
questions.explanation: Core concept: New DS-TB = 2HRZE + 4HRE daily regimen under NTEP. Why correct: India's NTEP (2020): daily regimen (no intermittent thrice-weekly). Intensive phase: 2 months (HRZE). Continuation: 4 months (HRE — ethambutol added throughout, unlike older WHO guidelines). Weight-band dosing, FDC tablets. Why A wrong: INH monotherapy = latent TB infection treatment (not active TB). Why C wrong: Rifampicin monotherapy → rapid resistance. Why D wrong: Inadequate duration/drugs. Clinical relevance: NIKSHAY: mandatory TB notification portal. Private sector must notify all TB cases. Bedaquiline: for MDR-TB. Treatment outcomes: cured, treatment completed, treatment failure, died, LTFU. Exam trap: India's NTEP now uses DAILY dosing (not thrice-weekly) and adds ethambutol throughout continuation phase. This is different from older RNTCP regimens frequently asked in exams. Memory aid: 'TB = 2HRZE + 4HRE = 6 months daily DOTS.' Reference: Park – NTEP.

questions.questionText: ASHA (Accredited Social Health Activist) under NHM is:
questions.optionB: A trained community health volunteer at village level — one per 1000 population (link between community and healthcare system)
questions.explanation: Core concept: ASHA = trained female community health activist, link worker at village level. Why correct: ASHA: female resident of village, married, 25-45 years, 10th pass. One per 1000 population (500 in tribal areas). Trained (23 modules). Role: facilitates access to health services, mobilizes community for immunization, ANC, institutional delivery (JSY), acts as DOTS provider, health educator. Incentive-based (performance-based payments). Why A wrong: Not a doctor — volunteer/activist. Why C wrong: Not a nurse. Why D wrong: Community-based, not hospital-based. Clinical relevance: ASHA, ANM, and AWW (Anganwadi Worker) form the 'three pillars' of community health in India. ASHA was introduced under NRHM (2005). Exam trap: ASHA = 1 per 1000 (villages). ANM = 1 per sub-centre (5000 population). AWW = 1 per Anganwadi centre (400-800 population). Memory aid: 'ASHA = A Social Health Activist per 1000.' Reference: Park – Healthcare Delivery.
questions.conceptTag: ASHA NHM community health activist 1000 population
